channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Cartels turn to social media to lure Americans into human smuggling as Texas enforces stricter laws
item.description: Thousands of people have been arrested under Texas’ human smuggling law. Now they face at least a decade in prison under sentencing guidelines that took effect this year.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandro Serrano, Graphics By Elijah Nicholson-Messmer, KPRC 2

item.title: Internal divisions doom Michigan Democrats in their final days of legislative control
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — Michigan Democrats, recently hailed as a model of electoral and legislative success, are ending their final days in complete control of the state government mired by divisions and outright rebellion that has stalled votes on key priorities.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA VOLMERT and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US diplomats and hostage envoy in Syria on first visit since Assad ouster
item.description: The first U.S. diplomats to visit Syria since President Bashar Assad's ouster earlier this month are in Damascus to hold talks with the country's new leadership and seek information on the whereabouts of missing American journalist Austin Tice.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Democratic governors hammered Trump before the election. Now they're hoping to work with him
item.description: A handful of prominent Democratic governors are quickly adjusting their approach to President-elect Donald Trump before he takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pentagon warns that a government shutdown will cost troops their pay over the holidays
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republicans’ decision to abandon a congressional spending plan will cost troops their paychecks over the holidays unless some agreement is reached before Friday's deadline to prevent a government shutdown, the Pentagon warned.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FAA bans drones in parts of New Jersey
item.description: The FAA warned that "deadly force" could be used against the drones if they pose an "imminent security threat.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken announces aid for Sudan and talks about AI risks at UN Security Council
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken announced $200 million in humanitarian aid for conflict-torn Sudan during what was likely his final trip to the United Nations while in office. With the U.N. Security Council more divided than ever, Blinken led two meetings of the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the debt ceiling debate as a government shutdown looms in Washington
item.description: A debate over the debt ceiling is at the center of a dispute over funding that is pushing the possibility of a federal government shutdown to the brink in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Meg Kinnard, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOJ seeks to block Jan. 6 defendants from attending Trump inauguration
item.description: Attorneys at the Department of Justice are urging federal judges to reject petitions from at least two Jan. 6 defendants who are asking that they be allowed to come back to the nation's capital for President-elect Trump's inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICE deportations catch up to Trump-era numbers in FY 2024 as Biden admin comes to a close
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) deportations caught up with Trump-era levels in FY 2024, according to new agency data released on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jeffries says bipartisan funding deal is ‘best path,’ but doesn’t rule out thinner bill
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) said Thursday the spending deal secured by the two parties is the “best path” for avoiding a government shutdown, but he did not rule out Democratic support for a smaller package to keep the lights on into next year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Where's Biden? Lame-duck president slammed for 'quiet quitting' amid major government funding battle
item.description: President Biden was blasted for "quiet quitting" and failing to lead his fellow Democrats amid the fiscal battle in Congress that could lead to a government shutdown just days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin officials noticed stamina issues in president's first few months in office: report
item.description: Current and former members of President Biden's White House told the Wall Street Journal that they saw evidence of Biden's waning energy even in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans from hurricane-ravaged states warn against stripping disaster aid from spending bill
item.description: Senate Republicans from regions affected by hurricanes and other storms are warning negotiators against stripping disaster funding from the stopgap spending bill that is suddenly in turmoil after President-elect Trump heavily criticized Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Lawmakers caught off guard by Trump debt ceiling demand
item.description: Lawmakers on both sides of the aisle appeared caught off guard Wednesday as President-elect Trump demanded that a debt ceiling hike be paired with a stopgap government funding bill, rejecting a sweeping bipartisan plan to avert a government shutdown this week.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries suggests Democrats will oppose a 'clean' funding bill after Johnson's plan falters
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) suggested Wednesday that Democrats will oppose any federal spending bill that strays from the bipartisan deal announced a day prior, accusing GOP leaders of reneging on the agreement and risking a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump said he wants to ban trans athletes from competing. The reality is more nuanced
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s campaign promise to ‘ban’ transgender athletes struck a chord with voters. It’s uncertain, however, what exactly Trump will do once his second term begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Dems complain 'President Musk' running the show on GOP spending drama
item.description: House Democrats are complaining over being kept in Washington, D.C., as lawmakers scramble to avoid a government shutdown just days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How much power would RFK Jr. have at HHS? A former health secretary weighs in
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is making the case with senators that he should lead Health and Human Services. Kathleen Sebelius, who had the job under Obama, explains the power and limits of the role.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gorenstein, MPR News

item.title: Trump's words of opposition stop a bipartisan budget deal in its tracks with Musk's help
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump delivered a likely death blow to bipartisan congressional budget negotiations, rejecting the measure as full of giveaways to Democrats after billionaire ally Elon Musk whipped up outrage toward the bill and cheered on Republican lawmakers who announced
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate passed a defense bill Wednesday that authorizes significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members, aims to counter China's growing power and boosts overall military spending to $895 billion while also stripping coverage of transgender medical treatments for chil
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Kash Patel 'salivating' to investigate Liz Cheney
item.description: "If Liz Cheney witness tampered, she deserves the FBI being all over her front lawn," O'Reilly said on NewsNation's "CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cuomo, NewsNation

item.title: Senate passes defense bill that will raise troop pay and aims to counter China's power
item.description: The Senate has passed a defense bill that authorize significant pay raises for junior enlisted service members and boost overall military spending to $895 billion. It also strips coverage of transgender medical treatments for children of military members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Paxton files motion to block auction of border wall materials
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton filed a motion Tuesday to stop the sale of pieces of the border wall.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Jones, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court will hear arguments over the law that could ban TikTok in the U.S. if it's not sold
item.description: The justices said Wednesday they will hear arguments Jan. 10 about whether the law impermissibly restricts speech in violation of the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gaetz defends ‘embarrassing’ past behavior, fires shot at Ethics report
item.description: Former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) is defending what he says is "embarrassing, though not criminal" past behavior and taking a shot at the House Ethics Committee, following reports that the panel voted to release its findings about him.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: GOP report recommends Liz Cheney be criminally investigated over Jan. 6 work
item.description: It’s a claim that if pursued would likely face significant roadblocks but that nonetheless comes as Trump has suggested members of the Jan. 6 panel should “go to jail” for their work.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Angie Craig chosen as top Democrat on U.S. House Agriculture Committee
item.description: Minnesota 2nd District Rep. Angie Craig was elected Tuesday to serve as the top Democrat on the U.S. House Agriculture Committee.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Florida GOP lawmaker files bill banning political flags in public buildings
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge rules Trump's conviction withstands Supreme Court immunity decision
item.description: A New York judge upheld a jury’s verdict that convicted President-elect Trump of a felony, ruling the outcome of the hush money case can withstand the Supreme Court’s new test for presidential immunity.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams' former chief advisor expects to be indicted: lawyer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ingrid Lewis-Martin, a former top aide to New York Mayor Eric Adams, expects to be indicted on criminal charges related to alleged improper gifts, her lawyer said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: McConnell urges Trump to reject isolationism
item.description: Outgoing Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) is calling on President-elect Trump to reject isolationists in the Republican Party who will argue for the United States to focus on China at the expense of Ukraine and other areas of the world.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5
